author: y4rr
content:
Closed APPROVE — closed by the combined dispatch under #1137 that also covered #1136's Dirichlet migration.

The original deferral was based on a false premise: 'no GPU cholesky / lgamma / digamma kernels.' GPU cholesky DOES exist (verified at Pass 5.B.3 — linalg::cholesky → cuSOLVER potrf). lgamma/digamma genuinely don't have GPU vectorized kernels, but they compose at tensor level so a future GPU kernel slot-fills transparently; for now they CPU-execute internally via unary_map.

Dirichlet Pattern B applied to 6 methods:
- log_prob: lgamma(sum(α)) - sum(lgamma(α)) + sum_dim((α-1) * log(x), -1) via tensor-level lgamma/digamma + grad-aware arithmetic
- mean: concentration / sum_dim(concentration, -1, keepdim=true)
- variance: composed sum_all/sub/mul/add/div over alpha + alpha_0 scalar
- entropy: composed scalar formula on device
- sample/rsample: scalar Gamma rejection sampling (intrinsic; no GPU Gamma kernel exists; result built directly via TensorStorage::on_device with no .to(device) round-trip)

All 6 fallback::check_gpu_fallback_opt_in gates stripped. All 6 out.to(device) recomposes stripped. Pattern B fully landed for the tensor-input hot paths.

See #1137 resolution comment for full evidence-floor outputs (369/369 lib + 60+41+1 integration tests pass; build + clippy clean; 2 sabotage probes captured + reverted).
